title: Fix live confidence gate rebreak so sub-0.60 signals cannot open after gate rejection

context.problem_statement: A fresh live audit confirmed that the current runtime can both emit confidence_too_low rejects at the 0.600 floor and still open sub-0.60 trades in the same live system. This is a real gate/open ordering inconsistency or bypass, not simple config drift.
context.manager_intent: Apply the smallest safe patch that guarantees a signal rejected by the confidence gate cannot proceed to open_position() through any live bridge path in the current flow.

objective: Find and fix the exact current live bridge path inconsistency so sub-0.60 signals cannot open after gate rejection, while preserving valid >=0.60 trade flow.
conditional_allowed_files: C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\core\model_bridge.py (ONLY if strictly required to eliminate duplicate/ordering issues at the source)
C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\core\risk.py (read-only unless a tiny compatibility patch is absolutely required and documented)
forbidden_actions: Read every file in the repo
required_work: Inspect the exact current gate/open ordering in bot_core.py for the live bridge flow
Identify how a signal can still reach open_position() after the 0.600 confidence gate should have rejected it
Patch the smallest safe point in the live bridge flow so a rejected signal cannot fall through to open_position()
If duplicate slug / repeated intent handling is part of the current live inconsistency, add the minimal per-iteration protection required
Preserve valid >=0.60 trade flow and existing logging style
acceptance_criteria: No live bridge path can call open_position() for a signal rejected by check_entry_gates()
Sub-0.60 signals cannot open after a confidence_too_low rejection in the same loop/runtime path
No files outside allowed scope are modified unless explicitly documented under conditional scope
If model_bridge.py is modified, python -m py_compile core/model_bridge.py passes
verification_required: Explain old behavior vs new behavior
State whether duplicate-intent / per-iteration handling was touched
Run py_compile on each changed Python file
State whether restart is required for the fix to take effect
